# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_admin_user! # Ensure only authenticated admin users can access this

  def index
    # Optionally display a form for entering SQL queries
  end

  def run
    @report_data = []
    @error = nil

    if params[:query].present?
      begin
        # WARNING: This approach allows direct execution of user-provided SQL.
        # It is extremely dangerous if not restricted to highly trusted power users (e.g., specific admins).
        # A malicious user could drop tables, delete data, or retrieve sensitive information.
        # Ensure your authentication and authorization are robust.
        result = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(params[:query])

        if result.is_a?(Array)
          # PG::Result for PostgreSQL, or similar for other adapters
          @report_data = result
          # If the result has column names (e.g., from SELECT), use them
          @column_names = result.try(:fields) || (result.first.keys if result.first.is_a?(Hash)) || []
        else
          # Handle cases where execute might return a non-array (e.g., for DDL statements)
          @report_data = [{ message: "Query executed successfully. Result type: #{result.class}" }]
          @column_names = ['message']
        end
      rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
        @error = "SQL Error: #{e.message}"
      rescue => e
        @error = "An unexpected error occurred: #{e.message}"
      end
    else
      @error = "No SQL query provided."
    end

    render :index # Render the index view to display results or errors
  end
end

# Example route in config/routes.rb:
# get 'reports', to: 'reports#index'
# post 'reports/run', to: 'reports#run', as: :run_reports

# Example index.html.erb view:
# <h1>Custom SQL Reports</h1>

# <form action="<%= run_reports_path %>" method="post">
#   <%= hidden_field_tag :authenticity_token, form_authenticity_token %>
#   <div style="margin-bottom: 1em;">
#     <label for="query">Enter SQL Query:</label><br>
#     <textarea name="query" id="query" rows="10" cols="80" style="width: 100%; font-family: monospace;"><%= params[:query] %></textarea>
#   </div>
#   <button type="submit">Run Query</button>
# </form>

# <% if @error %>
#   <p style="color: red;"><strong>Error:</strong> <%= @error %></p>
# <% elsif @report_data.present? %>
#   <h2>Query Results</h2>
#   <% if @report_data.first.is_a?(Hash) || @report_data.first.is_a?(ActiveSupport::HashWithIndifferentAccess) %>
#     <table border="1" style="width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse;">
#       <thead>
#         <tr>
#           <% (@column_names.presence || @report_data.first.keys).each do |col| %>
#             <th><%= col %></th>
#           <% end %>
#         </tr>
#       </thead>
#       <tbody>
#         <% @report_data.each do |row| %>
#           <tr>
#             <% (@column_names.presence || row.keys).each do |col| %>
#               <td><%= row[col] %></td>
#             <% end %>
#           </tr>
#         <% end %>
#       </tbody>
#     </table>
#   <% else %>
#     <p>Results are not in a standard hash/object format. Displaying raw:</p>
#     <pre><%= @report_data.inspect %></pre>
#   <% end %>
# <% end %>
